prompt:
Highly detailed 18th-century botanical scientific illustration plate depicting a branching plant specimen structured as a decision tree classification system.

Composition:
Single centered botanical specimen occupying approximately 70 percent of the page height, vertical orientation on aged off-white rag paper background with visible paper grain texture. Clean margins on all sides, specimen isolated with no environmental scenery.

Structure:
A single main stem rising from the base labeled "Root Node". The stem divides into multiple precise bifurcating branches at regular intervals, each branching point clearly defined as a decision split. Terminal branch tips end in distinct leaf forms representing classification outcomes.

Each branching junction is annotated with small serif text labels connected by thin straight leader lines, for example:
"Feature A > Threshold"
"Condition True"
"Condition False"

Terminal leaves are labeled with classification results such as:
"Class A"
"Class B"
"Class C"

Lines:
Extremely fine, controlled ink linework typical of copperplate botanical engraving. Consistent line thickness with delicate cross-hatching used sparingly to indicate form. No sketch lines, no painterly brush strokes.

Color:
Subtle hand-applied watercolor pigments in muted natural tones: soft sage green leaves, pale olive stems, light brown root structure. Slight tonal variation only within botanical realism. No saturated colors. No digital gradients. No glow or lighting effects.

Layout:
Formal scientific plate layout with the following elements:
Top center: Latin-style specimen title in small serif type "Arbor Decisionis".
Lower margin: Plate number in Roman numerals "Plate XII".
Bottom right: Scale reference bar labeled "Scale 1:1".
Bottom left: Publication-style credit text in small serif font "Illustrated for the Study of Classification Systems".
Spacing between text and specimen carefully maintained to reflect traditional natural history plate design.

Style:
Authentic 18th-century botanical natural history illustration, hand-colored engraving aesthetic, scientific taxonomy plate, museum-quality archival illustration.

Constraints:
Single frame only. No background scenery. No decorative borders. No modern symbols. No infographic styling. No diagram arrows. No computer interface elements.

Negative prompt:
photography, 3D rendering, digital illustration, modern typography, sans-serif font, neon colors, bright gradients, realistic lighting, shadows cast by light source, depth of field, lens effects, chart style, flowchart, UI design, icons, vector graphics, futuristic elements, AI art style
tradition_approach: Centered botanical specimen with fine copperplate linework, muted watercolor pigments, labeled branching structure, and formal scientific plate layout to mirror decision tree hierarchy.
